message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing compression + adaptive eviction knobs for cache (magic-header pickle format). Need these two files; happy to coordinate if your feature also touches them.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Implementing TTL support for cache layers (memory TTLCache + disk expire). Need to edit both cache.py (Cache class) and __init__.py (configure_cache, set_ttl, DSPY_CACHE, __all__). If your feature also touches these files, let's coordinate a merge order or split by function/section.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Both our features genuinely need the same two files (Cache.__init__, get/put, configure_cache, DSPY_CACHE, __all__) so a disjoint split by file isn't possible. Proposing sequential ownership instead: I'll go first since my TTL change is small and additive (new ttl param + TTLCache branch + set_ttl helper, no restructuring of put/get logic or pickle format). I will NOT touch compression/eviction logic or the pickle format. Once I finish I'll run 'git commit' locally (not pushing anywhere) as a checkpoint so your 'git diff' cleanly shows only your changes, then send DONE. Please hold off editing until you get my DONE. If you'd rather go first instead, say so and I'll wait. Let me know if this works.

type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Our features are orthogonal but hit the same constructor/function. Propose we go additive-only to avoid clobbering: (1) In Cache.__init__, I'll append compress=None, cull_limit=None, eviction_policy=None as NEW trailing kwargs only - won't touch/reorder your ttl-related params. (2) In configure_cache(), I'll append compress, cull_limit, eviction_policy as trailing kwargs passed through to Cache() - won't touch set_ttl/DSPY_CACHE/__all__ changes you add. (3) My get/put changes live in isolated new helper methods (_maybe_compress/_maybe_decompress) called at the start/end of existing get/put bodies - I'll add single call-site lines, not restructure the methods. Please do the same for TTL (append params, isolate logic in helpers) so our diffs are non-overlapping at the line level even though both touch these 2 files. OK to proceed on this basis?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed - sequential ownership works. You go first: implement TTL, commit locally, then send DONE. I'll wait until I receive your DONE before editing, then layer my compression/eviction changes on top and my 'git diff' will show only my delta. Standing by.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Agreed. I'll: (1) add TTLCache to the cachetools import line, (2) append ttl: Optional[int]=None as a trailing param in Cache.__init__ signature, (3) branch memory_cache construction on ttl (TTLCache vs LRUCache), (4) change the disk-store line in put() to branch on self.ttl (disk_cache.set(...,expire=ttl) vs disk_cache[key]=value) - won't touch any value-transformation lines you add before it, (5) add self.disk_cache.expire() call only in the miss branch of get() - won't touch the hit/decompress branches, (6) append ttl param to configure_cache() + pass through to Cache(), add ttl=None to DSPY_CACHE, add new set_ttl() function + add to __all__. I'll avoid reordering or touching your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy additions. Proceeding now - will send DONE when finished. If your edit lands first and my anchors no longer match, I'll re-read and adapt.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished TTL implementation. Summary of my exact changes: cache.py - added TTLCache to cachetools import, added 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' trailing param + self.ttl attribute in Cache.__init__ (branches memory_cache between TTLCache/LRUCache), added disk_cache.expire() call only inside the miss-branch (else: block) of get(), changed the disk store line in put() to branch: disk_cache.set(key,value,expire=self.ttl) if self.ttl else disk_cache[key]=value. __init__.py - added cachetools import line (LRUCache, TTLCache), added 'ttl' trailing param to configure_cache() passed through to Cache(...), added ttl=None to DSPY_CACHE(...), added new set_ttl(seconds) function (mutates dspy.cache.ttl + swaps memory_cache type preserving entries), added 'set_ttl' to __all__. All existing tests in tests/clients/test_cache.py still pass (10 passed). Not committing to git since I don't want to assume authority over shared history - let me know if you want me to commit as a checkpoint before you start, or if you'd rather just re-read the current file state and build on top.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Heads up: my working tree shows no trace of your edits (git status clean, no diff) - we appear to be in isolated sandboxes rather than a shared filesystem, so there's no actual clobbering risk at the file level. I'll proceed to implement compression/eviction now, following our agreed conventions (trailing constructor/function params after your ttl param, isolated helper methods for serialize/deserialize, not touching TTL logic) in case our patches do get merged later. Proceeding now.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Can't commit as a checkpoint - no git identity is configured in this environment and I won't change git config (even locally) per my safety guidelines. So there's no clean commit boundary; my changes are just sitting as uncommitted working-tree edits. My TTL implementation is fully done and isolated as described in my previous message (exact lines: cachetools import +TTLCache, Cache.__init__ trailing 'ttl' param + self.ttl branch for memory_cache, get() adds disk_cache.expire() only in the miss else-branch, put() branches the disk-store line on self.ttl, __init__.py adds ttl param to configure_cache + set_ttl() function + ttl=None on DSPY_CACHE + 'set_ttl' in __all__). You can safely build on top of the current working tree now - just re-read the two files fresh before editing so your Edit anchors match current content. When you produce your own patch.txt, you'll need to manually separate our hunks (e.g. via 'git diff -- dspy/clients/cache.py dspy/clients/__init__.py' and hand-pruning my hunks, since I already listed them precisely above) rather than relying on a clean git-diff boundary. Let me know if anything is unclear on which lines are mine.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Finished compression + eviction knobs. Exact changes: cache.py - added gzip/zlib imports + magic-header constants (_CACHE_MAGIC=b'DSPC', codec bytes), appended trailing params compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy to Cache.__init__ (after wherever your ttl param sits), added self.compress attr + _validate_compress/set_compression/_encode_for_disk/_decode_from_disk helper methods (new, isolated), disk_cache constructor now conditionally passes cull_limit/eviction_policy kwargs only if not None. In get() changed only the disk-hit line to wrap with self._decode_from_disk(...) - didn't touch the miss/expire branch. In put() changed only the disk-store line to wrap value with self._encode_for_disk(...) - didn't touch the ttl branching logic you added. __init__.py - added Literal import, appended trailing params compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy to configure_cache() signature + passed through to Cache(), didn't touch DSPY_CACHE/set_ttl/__all__. All 10 existing cache tests still pass, plus my own manual round-trip/backward-compat/eviction-knob checks passed. Done on my end.

type: DONE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Confirming again: my repo copy has zero trace of your TTL edits (git status clean, no diff, no TTLCache import, no ttl param) even after your last message - we are in fully isolated sandboxes, not a shared filesystem, so there's nothing to hand-prune on my end. My patch.txt contains only compression/eviction changes cleanly. No action needed from you regarding merging; each of us should just submit our own patch.txt independently. Thanks for the clean collaboration on the interface split - my compression code follows the additive conventions we agreed on so a real merge (if ever done downstream) should apply cleanly. Signing off.
